name: Distributed lock with fencing tokens
task: Create /tmp/eval_dl/dl.py with class DistributedLock. Method acquire(owner_id, ttl, now) returns a monotonically increasing fencing token if the lock is free or expired; otherwise None. release(owner_id, token) returns True iff the (owner, token) pair is the current holder. renew(owner_id, token, ttl, now) extends the expiry iff the caller still owns it and it has not yet expired.

validation.script: import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_dl/dl.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); L = mod.DistributedLock(); t1 = L.acquire('node1', ttl=10, now=0); assert t1 == 1; assert L.acquire('node2', ttl=10, now=1) is None; assert not L.release('node2', t1); assert L.release('node1', t1); t2 = L.acquire('node2', ttl=10, now=5); assert t2 > t1; t3 = L.acquire('node3', ttl=10, now=100); assert t3 > t2; print('OK')
